evals.prompt: The implementation looks different from the original — run a quick visual check between https://example.com and http://localhost:3000
evals.expected_output: Quick mode: computed-diff → batch-scroll → AE comparison → DSSIM. All axes report PASS or specific FAIL positions identified with diff images.
expectations: computed-diff.sh ran FIRST with CSS reset canaries (h1, h2, body, header, footer)
batch-scroll.sh captured both original and impl at 0–100% scroll positions
batch-compare.sh ran AE comparison on all captured pairs
dssim-compare.sh ran structural similarity comparison
FAIL positions identified with AE > 500 threshold
Only diff images were Read for FAIL positions — ref/impl images were NOT Read

evals.prompt: Run full verification between https://example.com and http://localhost:3000 — I need to be sure everything matches before shipping
evals.expected_output: Full verification: Phase A (capture) → Phase C (AE+DSSIM+computed) → Phase D (pixel-perfect gate) → Phase E (LLM review). All phases pass.
expectations: Phase A: reference and impl screenshots captured at matching scroll positions
Phase C: AE, DSSIM, and computed-diff all executed
Phase D: pixel-perfect-diff.json produced with result: pass and mismatches: 0
Phase E: LLM review read ref+impl pairs for ALL positions and judged PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL
Three-axis verification applied: pixel (AE) + perceptual (DSSIM) + semantic (LLM)
A position only marked PASS when all three axes agree

evals.prompt: The hero section AE diff fails but I can't tell what's wrong from the diff image. Run computed-diff to find the root cause.
evals.expected_output: computed-diff.sh identifies exact CSS property mismatches (e.g., fontWeight, padding, backgroundColor) with ref vs impl values. Root cause named before any code fix.
expectations: computed-diff.sh ran with selectors targeting the hero section elements
Output shows per-property comparison: property, ref value, impl value, match/mismatch
Root cause identified as specific CSS property mismatch (not 'looks different')
No pixel comparison needed — computed-diff provided the diagnosis directly
Fix was targeted to the identified property, not a broad rewrite

evals.prompt: Run section-level comparison for my clone at http://localhost:3000 vs https://example.com — I need per-section pass/fail
evals.expected_output: section-compare.sh produces per-section AE + structure diff. Each section reports PASS or FAIL with specific issues.
expectations: computed-diff.sh ran first with section-specific selectors
section-compare.sh ran with all 4 arguments including ref dir path
Per-section results reported: section name, AE score, structure match, status
SVG-as-text mismatches detected if present
Layout type mismatches (flex vs grid) detected if present
result.txt written to the correct ref dir path for the Stop gate to read

evals.prompt: The batch comparison shows 3 FAIL positions at 30%, 50%, and 70% scroll. Diagnose and fix each one.
evals.expected_output: Each FAIL position diagnosed individually. Diff images read only for FAIL positions. Targeted fixes applied. Re-comparison passes.
expectations: Only diff images for 30%, 50%, 70% were Read — not ref/impl images
Each FAIL diagnosed with a specific root cause before fixing
Fixes applied one at a time, not all at once
After each fix, re-comparison ran for the affected position
All 3 positions pass on re-comparison
PASS positions were not re-examined or re-captured

evals.prompt: AE comparison passes but I suspect the colors are slightly off — run DSSIM to check perceptual similarity
evals.expected_output: dssim-compare.sh detects perceptual color/tone mismatches that AE missed. Specific positions flagged with DSSIM scores below threshold.
expectations: dssim-compare.sh ran on all captured pairs
DSSIM scores reported per position
Positions below SSIM 0.995 threshold flagged as FAIL
Color/tone mismatches identified that AE comparison missed
Three-axis principle applied: AE PASS + DSSIM FAIL = overall FAIL

evals.prompt: Phase E LLM review — read all ref+impl pairs and judge each position
evals.expected_output: LLM reads every position's ref+impl pair. Each position judged PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL with specific observations. Missing sections or wrong content caught.
expectations: Every position's ref AND impl images read as pairs
Each position explicitly judged: PASS, PARTIAL, or FAIL
Specific observations noted for each position (not just 'looks good')
Missing sections or structural differences caught that AE/DSSIM missed
PARTIAL positions explain what differs and whether it's acceptable
Overall summary aggregates all position judgments

evals.prompt: The self-healing loop: AE fails at 50%, I fix it, but now 40% fails. Fix that too — max 3 iterations total.
evals.expected_output: Phase H self-healing: fix 50% → re-compare → 40% fails → fix 40% → re-compare → all pass. Completed within 3 iterations.
expectations: Iteration 1: 50% FAIL diagnosed, fix applied, re-comparison ran
Iteration 1 re-compare: 50% now PASS, but 40% newly FAIL (regression detected)
Iteration 2: 40% FAIL diagnosed, fix applied without breaking 50%
Iteration 2 re-compare: both 40% and 50% PASS
Total iterations ≤ 3 (hard limit enforced)
If iteration 3 still had failures, escalated to user instead of continuing

evals.prompt: Compare hover transitions between original and implementation — the card hover timing feels different
evals.expected_output: transition-compare.sh captures idle/hover screenshots and computedStyle for each element. Timing diff identifies duration/easing mismatches.
expectations: transition-compare.sh ran with original and impl URLs
Idle state screenshots captured for both original and impl
Hover state screenshots captured for both original and impl
computedStyle diff ran for transitioned properties (transform, opacity, box-shadow)
Duration and easing values compared between original and impl
Specific timing mismatches identified (e.g., 300ms vs 200ms, ease-out vs linear)

evals.prompt: The implementation has a Lottie animation that causes AE false positives — every scroll position shows AE > 500 because the Lottie frame differs. Handle it.
evals.expected_output: Dynamic content threshold applied: AE bumped to 2000 for positions with Lottie. Lottie regions masked or threshold-adjusted. Remaining positions use standard AE ≤ 500.
expectations: Lottie animation identified as source of AE false positives
AE threshold bumped to 2000 for affected positions (not globally)
Non-Lottie positions still use standard AE ≤ 500 threshold
batch-compare.sh ran with dynamic thresholds or region masks
Actual layout/style issues not masked by the threshold adjustment
User informed about the threshold adjustment and why

evals.prompt: Run layout-diff to check if the section bounding boxes match between original and implementation
evals.expected_output: layout-diff.sh compares section bounding boxes. Height/width/position mismatches identified per section.
expectations: layout-diff.sh ran with session, original URL, and impl URL
Per-section bounding box comparison: top, height, width
Height mismatches flagged with exact pixel differences
Width mismatches flagged (especially for overflow issues)
Position drift detected (sections shifted vertically)
Results usable as pre-filter before detailed AE comparison

evals.prompt: pixel-perfect-diff.json shows 2 mismatches: header padding-top (60px vs 40px) and footer font-size (14px vs 16px). Fix both.
evals.expected_output: Both mismatches fixed with targeted CSS edits. Re-measurement confirms exact match. pixel-perfect-diff.json updated to result: pass, mismatches: 0.
expectations: Diff table shows ❌ for padding-top (60px vs 40px) and font-size (14px vs 16px)
CSS rules controlling both properties identified in implementation code
Targeted fix applied — only the affected properties changed
Re-measurement ran for both affected elements after fix
Both rows updated to ✅ in diff table
pixel-perfect-diff.json saved with result: pass and mismatches: 0

evals.prompt: All automated checks pass but something still looks off at the hero section. Run Phase E to catch what AE/DSSIM missed.
evals.expected_output: Phase E LLM review catches semantic issue invisible to pixel metrics — e.g., wrong text content, missing icon, swapped image. Issue documented and fixed.
expectations: Phase E ran even though AE and DSSIM both passed
LLM review identified a semantic issue (content, structure, or meaning) not pixel-level
Issue described with specific observation (not 'looks slightly different')
Fix applied for the semantic issue
Re-comparison confirmed the fix resolved the issue
Three-axis principle enforced: AE PASS + DSSIM PASS + LLM FAIL = overall FAIL

evals.prompt: Run visual debug but the original site is behind Cloudflare bot detection — screenshots show a challenge page instead of content
evals.expected_output: Bot detection detected on reference screenshots. Retried with increased wait. On failure, reported to user with options.
expectations: Bot detection detected on reference screenshot (challenge page, not content)
Retried capture with increased wait time (up to 2 retries)
If still blocked, reported to user with clear options: retry, manual capture, or provide screenshots
Did not proceed with comparison using bot-detection screenshots as reference
Did not crash or hang indefinitely

evals.prompt: After all visual checks pass, make sure browser sessions are cleaned up before finishing
evals.expected_output: All agent-browser sessions opened during comparison are closed by name. No orphaned Chrome Helper processes.
expectations: Every agent-browser session opened during this run is closed with --session <name> close
Cleanup runs before returning control to the user
Cleanup runs even on error or early exit
Never used close --all (would kill other sessions)
Only sessions owned by this comparison run are closed
